sections.paragraph:
προσβαίνω 2 aor. προσέβην (Hom., Hdt., Trag. X., ins, pap; 1 Esdr 4:53; 8:1; Jdth 4:7; 7:10; Joseph.) approach in our lit. only abs. (Soph., Phil. 42) προσέβην μικρόν I drew a little closer Hv 4, 1, 5.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

abs. abs. = absolute

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 876
